refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the latency metric and end-to-end scope clarifies the performance requirement and safety context.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of teleoperation commands and telemetry over the network link under defined network conditions during system integration testing.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control systems.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link to prevent latency spikes.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes near the vehicle to reduce network hops.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams via QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.

potential_sources: Dependency on network link quality and configuration
Interaction with safety alert messaging timing
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates teleoperation communication protocol design, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of latency spikes due to unexpected network congestion or failures.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met in initial implementation.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication protocols, command processing, and telemetry handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time vehicle status and sensor data sent to operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator commands received for vehicle control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of teleoperation latency logic; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module handles physical actuation but less relevant to latency control.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not control latency.
Overall system module with broad scope, less focused containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control logic is best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface latency if Teleoperation module depends heavily on Vehicle module processing.
Risk of duplicated latency handling if other modules implement overlapping communication logic.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts and avoid latency-related logic duplication across modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (dedicated low-latency communication protocols) directly addresses the refined FR's latency requirement with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as latency logic is centralized in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best ownership candidate for DP implementation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end via telemetry and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link quality closely to validate assumptions.
Review potential latency impact of Safety Services alerts on teleoperation communication.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the FR is not yet atomic and further refinement of sub-FRs is needed to fully capture latency contributors and verification methods.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control systems.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link to prevent latency spikes.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to the operator within latency constraints.

revise.why: To decompose the latency requirement into atomic sub-FRs for clearer DP and IM mapping and verification.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement dedicated protocols without excessive coupling.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network congestion.
Potential interface delays between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative network conditions.
Interface timing analysis between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
